questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai is widely regarded as the father of which national programme?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai is honoured as the father of the Indian space programme for founding and shaping ISRO.

questions.q: Which space organisation did Vikram Sarabhai play the leading role in founding?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai was instrumental in establishing the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) in 1969.

questions.q: In which Indian city did Vikram Sarabhai establish the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL)?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai founded the Physical Research Laboratory in Ahmedabad in 1947.

questions.q: What was Vikram Sarabhai's primary field of scientific training?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai was trained as a physicist and did research in cosmic rays.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Vikram Sarabhai born?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai was born in Ahmedabad in the state of Gujarat in 1919.

questions.q: Which kind of natural high-energy radiation did Sarabhai study for his doctoral research?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai's research focused on cosmic rays, the high-energy particles arriving from space.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai's family was well known for being prominent in which area of business?
questions.explanation: The Sarabhai family were leading industrialists in the textile business in Ahmedabad.

questions.q: ISRO's launch centre at Thumba and many institutions honour Sarabhai because he is seen as a pioneer of what?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai pioneered Indian space research, which is why ISRO centres carry his legacy.

questions.q: Sarabhai believed space technology should be used to solve the real problems of which group of people?
options: The common people of the nation
questions.explanation: Sarabhai argued that space technology should serve the genuine problems of ordinary people and national development.

questions.q: Vikram Sarabhai was married to a celebrated figure from which art form?
questions.explanation: He was married to Mrinalini Sarabhai, a renowned classical dancer.

questions.q: In which year was the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) founded by Sarabhai?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai established the PRL in Ahmedabad in 1947, the year of India's independence.

questions.q: Sarabhai founded an Ahmedabad centre, rooted in his 1966 earth station, that became which ISRO research centre?
options: ISRO Telemetry, Tracking and Command Network
questions.explanation: Sarabhai set up an experimental satellite communication earth station in Ahmedabad in 1966; its units later merged in 1972 to form ISRO's Space Applications Centre (SAC).

questions.q: In which year was the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) formed under Sarabhai's leadership?
questions.explanation: ISRO was established in 1969, with Sarabhai as a driving force in its creation.

questions.q: Sarabhai helped set up India's first rocket launching station at Thumba. Near which city is Thumba located?
questions.explanation: The Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station (TERLS) is near Thiruvananthapuram in Kerala.

questions.q: Thumba was chosen as a rocket launching site largely because of its proximity to what?
questions.explanation: Thumba lies close to the geomagnetic equator, making it ideal for upper-atmosphere and ionospheric studies.

questions.q: Sarabhai conceived a landmark project to broadcast educational programmes to villages via satellite. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai envisioned the Satellite Instructional Television Experiment (SITE) to bring educational TV to rural India.

questions.q: After whose death in 1966 did Sarabhai become chairman of India's Atomic Energy Commission?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai succeeded Homi J. Bhabha as chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission after Bhabha's death in 1966.

questions.q: Which prestigious Indian civilian honour was conferred on Vikram Sarabhai?
options: Padma Bhushan and the posthumous Padma Vibhushan
questions.explanation: Sarabhai received the Padma Bhushan in 1966 and was awarded the Padma Vibhushan posthumously in 1972.

questions.q: Sarabhai was a co-founder of which premier Indian management institute?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai was a key co-founder of the Indian Institute of Management (IIM) Ahmedabad in 1961.

questions.q: In which year did Vikram Sarabhai pass away?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai died on 30 December 1971 at Kovalam in Kerala.

questions.q: ISRO's lead launch-vehicle centre in Thiruvananthapuram, the former Space Science and Technology Centre at Thumba, is named after Sarabhai as the:
questions.explanation: The Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC) in Thiruvananthapuram, ISRO's lead launch-vehicle development centre, is named in his memory; the Bengaluru satellite centre (ISAC) is instead named the U R Rao Satellite Centre.

questions.q: Which research institute focused on textiles did Sarabhai help establish in Ahmedabad?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai was instrumental in founding the Ahmedabad Textile Industry's Research Association (ATIRA) in 1947.

questions.q: At which university did Sarabhai earn his PhD, with a thesis on cosmic ray investigations?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai earned his doctorate from the University of Cambridge for work on cosmic ray investigations in tropical latitudes.

questions.q: Sarabhai's first sounding rocket was launched from Thumba in 1963. From which country was that rocket?
questions.explanation: India's first sounding rocket, an American Nike-Apache, was launched from Thumba on 21 November 1963.

questions.q: Before ISRO, India's space activities were guided by a national committee Sarabhai chaired from 1962. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai chaired the Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR), set up in 1962 as the precursor to ISRO.

questions.q: The SITE satellite-TV experiment Sarabhai championed was eventually carried out in 1975-76 using a satellite from which space agency?
questions.explanation: SITE used NASA's ATS-6 satellite to beam educational television to thousands of Indian villages in 1975-76.

questions.q: Sarabhai's doctoral and early cosmic-ray work began at which Indian institution before he founded PRL?
questions.explanation: Sarabhai began cosmic-ray studies at the Indian Institute of Science in Bangalore under C. V. Raman before completing his PhD.

questions.q: Which well-known textile-and-design institution in Ahmedabad did Sarabhai help found in 1961?
options: Sir J. J. School of Art
Faculty of Fine Arts, MSU Baroda
questions.explanation: Sarabhai was a founding figure behind the National Institute of Design (NID) in Ahmedabad in 1961.

questions.q: A prominent lunar feature on the Moon's near side was named 'Sarabhai' in his honour. What type of feature is it?
questions.explanation: The crater 'Sarabhai' on the Moon, located in Mare Serenitatis, was named to honour Vikram Sarabhai.

questions.q: India's lander as part of Chandrayaan-2 (2019) was named to honour Sarabhai. What was the lander called?
questions.explanation: The Chandrayaan-2 lander was named 'Vikram' in honour of Vikram Sarabhai, the father of the Indian space programme.
